name: AT&T Stadium
description: The largest venue of the tournament, famous for its giant retractable roof and video board.

name: Estadio Azteca
description: Football's most storied stadium, the first ever to host matches at three different World Cups.

name: MetLife Stadium
description: Shared home of the Giants and Jets, set to host the World Cup final on July 19.

name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
description: One of the most technologically advanced stadiums in the world.

name: GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium
description: Holds the Guinness World Record for loudest crowd roar at a sports stadium.

name: NRG Stadium
description: Home of the NFL's Houston Texans, with a retractable roof keeping Houston's heat at bay.

name: Levi's Stadium
description: A modern Silicon Valley venue that underwent a $200 million renovation for the tournament.

name: SoFi Stadium
description: A striking modern venue, home of the LA Rams and Chargers.

name: Lincoln Financial Field
description: Known as 'The Linc', home of the NFL's Philadelphia Eagles.

name: Lumen Field
description: Renowned for its deafening crowd noise, home of the Seattle Seahawks.

name: Hard Rock Stadium
description: Set to host the third-place play-off, home of the Miami Dolphins.

name: Gillette Stadium
description: Recognizable for its 218-foot signature lighthouse and 360-degree observation deck.

name: BC Place
description: Canada's largest stadium, with a retractable roof shielding fans from Pacific Northwest weather.

name: Estadio BBVA
description: Known as 'The Steel Giant', offering views of the surrounding mountains.

name: BMO Field
description: One of the few purpose-built football grounds in the tournament, with steep stands close to the pitch.

name: Estadio Akron
description: A striking coliseum-style stadium with a distinctive volcano-inspired design.
